info.title: TicketRush API
info.description: OpenAPI specification for TicketRush aligned with the completed PostgreSQL migration. The API covers Customer and Admin flows: authentication, event management, event images, seat sections and generated seat matrices, row-lock based seat locking with 10-minute expiry, mock checkout, per-seat electronic tickets with QR codes, polling/SSE seat-map updates, virtual waiting queue, dashboard statistics, and audit logs.

tags.name: Users
tags.description: User profile and admin user management

tags.name: Seats
tags.description: Seat map, seat status, and seat generation

tags.name: Tickets
tags.description: Electronic ticket and QR code APIs

post.description: Locks seats for the authenticated customer for 10 minutes. Backend must run this flow inside a database transaction, lock the selected seats with row-level locking, verify each seat status is Available/Released, update seats to Locked, insert seat_locks with status Active and expires_at = now() + interval '10 minutes', and rely on the partial unique index uq_active_seat_lock to prevent two Active locks on the same seat.

post.description: Releases all Active seat_locks where expires_at < now(), marks locks Expired, and changes seats from Locked to Released/Available depending on implementation. This can be called manually by Admin or by a scheduled worker.

post.description: Creates a Pending order from seats that are currently Active-locked by the authenticated user. Backend writes order_items.price_snapshot from seat_sections.price and stores orders.total_amount.

post.description: Confirms mock payment. Backend validates that all order seats are still locked by the current user and not expired, sets order status to Paid, seats to Sold, seat_locks to Paid, and creates one ticket per order_item with unique qr_code and issued_at.

get.summary: Polling API for seat-map status changes
get.description: Use this endpoint for polling-based realtime updates. Client sends the last seen timestamp and receives seats whose status changed after that timestamp.

409.description: Conflict, for example seat already locked or sold

get.summary: SSE stream for seat-map status changes
get.description: Optional Server-Sent Events endpoint. Use either this SSE endpoint, WebSocket, or polling endpoint. Event payload should match SeatMapChange. WebSocket alternative: /ws/events/{eventId}/seat-map.

code.description: Derived display code from section, rowNumber and seatNumber; not a database column.

price.description: Derived from seat_sections.price, not stored directly in seats table.

status.description: Seat lock lifecycle. Only one Active lock per seat is allowed by partial unique index.

SeatLock.description: Represents a temporary seat hold. Backend must use a database transaction/row lock when creating Active locks. A seat lock expires after 10 minutes unless checkout marks it Paid or user/background worker releases it.

queueToken.description: Required when virtual waiting queue is enabled. Maps to waiting_queue.token.

priceSnapshot.description: Price copied from seat_sections.price when the order item is created.

Order.description: Order table row. totalAmount is stored as a snapshot at checkout/order creation time, not recalculated from current section prices.

admissionToken.description: Deprecated alias. Use token instead; database column is waiting_queue.token.

token.description: Admission token stored in waiting_queue.token when entry becomes Admitted.
